acronyms.AAPE: Average absolute percentage error, a regression fit-quality metric.
acronyms.ANNIE: The Schoenberg-Muir-Sayers (1996) simplified three-parameter transversely isotropic velocity model for shales, behind the annie_c11/annie_c13 stiffness approximations.
acronyms.API: American Petroleum Institute; API units calibrate gamma-ray logs (see the non-SI units table) and API gravity grades crude oils.
acronyms.BET: Brunauer-Emmett-Teller multilayer gas-adsorption isotherm, the basis of specific-surface-area measurement.
acronyms.BI: Bond index: cement-bond quality normalized to [0, 1] between the free-pipe (0) and well-bonded (1) anchors.
acronyms.BPP: Bloembergen-Purcell-Pound theory of NMR relaxation by molecular motion.
acronyms.BVI: Bulk volume irreducible: the NMR porosity fraction below the T2 cutoff, held as capillary-bound water.
acronyms.BVW: Bulk volume water: porosity multiplied by water saturation.
acronyms.C/O: Carbon-oxygen ratio from inelastic neutron spectroscopy, an oil-saturation indicator independent of water salinity.
acronyms.C1-C5: Light-hydrocarbon components in mud-gas analysis: C1 methane, C2 ethane, C3 propane, C4 butanes, C5 pentanes.
acronyms.CBL: Cement bond log: the cased-hole sonic amplitude/attenuation measurement of casing-to-cement bond quality.
acronyms.CBW: Clay-bound water: the shortest-T2 fraction of the NMR porosity spectrum.
acronyms.CEC: Cation exchange capacity of clay (typically meq/g), the source of excess conductivity in shaly-sand models.
acronyms.CFL: Courant-Friedrichs-Lewy stability number limiting the time step of explicit finite-difference schemes.
acronyms.CGR: Computed (uranium-free) gamma ray built from the potassium and thorium contributions only.
acronyms.Cij: Elastic stiffness-tensor components in Voigt notation (C11, C33, C44, C66, C13, C12).
acronyms.CODATA: Committee on Data for Science and Technology; source of the recommended values of the physical constants used in petrolib.constants.
acronyms.CPMG: Carr-Purcell-Meiboom-Gill multi-echo pulse sequence used to measure NMR T2.
acronyms.CRIM: Complex refractive index method, a volumetric mixing law for dielectric permittivity.
acronyms.CT: (X-ray) computed tomography of core; its attenuation contrast maps to porosity and saturation.
acronyms.DC: Direct current, i.e. the zero-frequency limit (e.g. the DC resistivity rho0 of Cole-Cole dispersion).
acronyms.DLIS: Digital Log Interchange Standard wireline data format.
acronyms.DOI: Digital object identifier of a journal article.
acronyms.DTW: Dynamic time warping, the elastic depth-alignment algorithm for log curves.
acronyms.ECD: Equivalent circulating density: effective mud density including annular friction losses while circulating.
acronyms.FFI: Free fluid index: the NMR porosity fraction above the T2 cutoff, i.e. producible fluid.
acronyms.FHZ: Flory-Huggins-Zuo equation of state for asphaltene concentration gradients.
acronyms.FZI: Flow zone indicator: RQI/phi_z, the Amaefule-Tiab hydraulic rock-typing variable.
acronyms.GLCM: Gray-level co-occurrence matrix, a texture descriptor for borehole images.
acronyms.HI: Hydrogen index: hydrogen density relative to fresh water.
acronyms.IEEE: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers; its standard 754 defines the binary floating-point arithmetic assumed by the regression tests.
acronyms.IGR: Gamma-ray index (GR - GR_clean)/(GR_shale - GR_clean), the linear input to shale-volume transforms.
acronyms.IQR: Interquartile range (Q3 - Q1), a robust spread measure.
acronyms.ISTA: Iterative shrinkage-thresholding algorithm for L1-regularized (sparse) inversion.
acronyms.L1 / L2: Sum-of-absolute-values and Euclidean (sum-of-squares) vector norms used in misfits and regularization.
acronyms.LAS: Log ASCII Standard wireline data format.
acronyms.LM: Levenberg-Marquardt damped least-squares algorithm for nonlinear fitting.
acronyms.LOM: Level of organic metamorphism: the thermal-maturity scale in Passey's Delta-log-R TOC method.
acronyms.MAD: Median absolute deviation, a robust scale estimate used for despiking.
acronyms.MALA: Metropolis-adjusted Langevin algorithm, a gradient-informed MCMC sampler.
acronyms.MAP: Maximum a posteriori: the mode of a Bayesian posterior distribution.
acronyms.MCMC: Markov chain Monte Carlo posterior sampling.
acronyms.MW: Mud weight (in the drilling gas-normalization formulas).
acronyms.NCS: Net confining stress on a core sample.
acronyms.ND: Neutron-density shale/clay indicator built from the neutron-porosity and density logs.
acronyms.OB: Overburden (vertical total) stress or pressure, as in the Eaton pore-pressure relation.
acronyms.OD: Optical density (asphaltene gradients); also outer diameter (casing condition).
acronyms.OSI: Oil saturation index: Rock-Eval S1 divided by TOC, in mg hydrocarbon per g TOC.
acronyms.RF: Ratio factor of the minimum-curvature well-trajectory method.
acronyms.RFN: Rock fabric number in Lucia's carbonate permeability classification.
acronyms.RI: Resistivity index Rt/R0 in Archie's second law.
acronyms.S/V: Pore surface-to-volume ratio, which controls NMR surface relaxation.
acronyms.SDR: Schlumberger-Doll Research NMR permeability transform k = C*phi^4*T2LM^2.
acronyms.SG: Specific gravity: density relative to fresh water (liquids) or to air (gases).
acronyms.SGR: Spectral (total) gamma ray reconstructed from the K, U, and Th concentrations.
acronyms.SI: The International System of Units; petrolib's default unit convention.
acronyms.SPWLA: Society of Petrophysicists and Well Log Analysts, publisher of the journal Petrophysics.
acronyms.T2: Transverse (spin-spin) NMR relaxation time; T2LM is its amplitude-weighted logarithmic mean.
acronyms.TE: Inter-echo spacing of the CPMG pulse sequence.
acronyms.TMALI: Thermal Maturity-Adjusted Log Interpretation, the Craddock et al. (2019) organic-shale workflow.
acronyms.TP / FP / TN / FN: True/false positive/negative counts of a binary classification.
acronyms.ULP: Unit in the last place: the spacing between adjacent floating-point numbers.
acronyms.USBM: U.S. Bureau of Mines wettability index.
acronyms.VMD: Variational mode decomposition, a signal-denoising method.
acronyms.VTI: Vertical transverse isotropy: a layered medium with a vertical axis of rotational symmetry.

units.API unit (gAPI): Gamma-ray calibration unit of the American Petroleum Institute, defined so the contrast of the API test pit in Houston reads 200 units; gamma-ray logs are recorded in gAPI.
Capture unit of the thermal-neutron capture cross-section Sigma: 1 c.u. = 1e-3 per cm.
units.darcy (D), millidarcy (mD): Permeability: 1 darcy = 9.869233e-13 m2 (about 1 um2); 1 mD = 1e-3 D.
units.ft: Foot: 1 ft = 0.3048 m exactly (also ft/s velocities, ft/hr penetration rates, and psi/ft gradients).
units.g/cc: Gram per cubic centimetre (CGS density): 1 g/cc = 1000 kg/m3.
Inch: 1 in. = 25.4 mm exactly (bit and casing diameters).
units.M (molar): Mole per litre of solution: 1 M = 1000 mol/m3.
units.meq/g, meq/ml: Milliequivalent (1e-3 mol of unit charge) per gram or per millilitre; the units of CEC and of Qv in shaly-sand conductivity models.
units.MRayl: Megarayl, specific acoustic impedance: 1 MRayl = 1e6 rayl, where 1 rayl = 1 Pa*s/m = 1 kg/(m2*s).
Porosity unit: one percentage point of porosity (0.01 v/v).
units.ppg: Pound per US gallon (mud weight): 1 ppg = 119.83 kg/m3, equivalent to a hydrostatic gradient of 0.052 psi/ft.
units.ppm: Part per million (fraction 1e-6), e.g. NaCl salinity or trace-element concentration.
units.psi: Pound-force per square inch: 1 psi = 6894.757 Pa; the gradient 1 psi/ft = 22.62 kPa/m.
units.us/ft: Microsecond per foot, the sonic slowness convention: 1 us/ft = 3.2808 us/m; velocity in ft/s is 1e6 divided by the slowness.
units.v/v: Volume fraction (dimensionless); the porosity and saturation convention.
